description_long: This stone object is roughly hemispherical with a highly textured, convoluted surface resembling brain coral or intricate natural erosion patterns. The upper and side surfaces are marked by deep grooves and ridges, creating a visually complex and tactilely rough appearance. One side is smoother and less detailed, suggesting partial weathering or breakage, while the rest of the stone maintains its rugged, organic look. The coloration is predominantly a pale, off-white to light beige, with subtle variations in shading that accentuate the surface relief. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its irregular form and pronounced surface features make it a striking natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: A pale, hemispherical stone with deep, brain-like grooves and a rugged, textured surface. One side is smoother, contrasting with the intricate relief of the rest.
description_view_0: The top view highlights the dense network of deep grooves and ridges, giving the stone a brain-like appearance.
description_view_1: This side view shows a transition from the highly textured surface to a smoother, less detailed area, suggesting partial weathering.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the smoothest and least detailed surface, with minimal grooves and a more uniform texture.
description_view_3: This angled view emphasizes the contrast between the rugged, grooved surface and the smoother section, showing the stone's irregular form.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Deep, irregular grooves and ridges forming a natural, brain-like pattern with high visual complexity.
attribute_weathering: Rough and eroded with some smoother areas, indicating moderate natural weathering and exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, often used as a focal point or naturalistic barrier in landscaping.
